# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes deployment YAML for our application stack. Include deployments for web application, Memcached cache, and background workers. Define services to expose Memcached for inter-pod communication. This is for our production Kubernetes cluster.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for memcached exposed port and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: memcached_exposed_port

apiVersion: apps/v1  # Kubernetes API Version for Deployment and Service resources  
kind: Deployment    # Kind of resource to create (Deployment)
metadata:             # Metadata associated with the deployment, such as name & namespace.
  labels:             
    app: webapp         # Labels are used by kubectl commands like 'kubectl get pods' for filtering resources based on label selectors  
      tier: frontend     # Tags can be added to the resource, making it easier in selecting and managing them.  For example - `tier=frontend` would allow us only list out all Pods with 'app = webapp', which is more suitable for our case as we have multiple applications running on one cluster
      environment: prod   # Tags can be added to the resource, making it easier in selecting and managing them.  For example - `environment=prod` would allow us only list out all Pods with 'tier = frontend' which is more suitable for our case as we have multiple applications running on one cluster
      version: v1       # Tags can be added to the resource, making it easier in selecting and managing them.  For example - `version=v1` would allow us only list out all Pods with 'environment = prod' which is more suitable for our case as we have multiple applications running on one cluster
    kubernetes: "true"   # This tells K8S to manage the resource, and not create it.  It will handle scaling of pod replicas etc by default in a real production environment    
specs:                # Specifications associated with deployment such as number & type of Pod(s) that should be created for this application/deployment  
    selector:          # Selector is used to match the Deployments and Services.  It'll select all pod which have 'app=webApp', tier = frontend, environment =  prod , version  = v1 label set by us    
      matchLabels:      
        app: webapp         # Matches with labels defined in metadata section of the Deployment resource  
          tier: frontend   
            environment: prod 
              kubernetes: "true"          
             type : LoadBalancer# This will expose your service to outside world. It can be either a Service or Ingress depending on use case    
      template:         # Template is used by K8S for creating Pods  
        metadata:       # Metadata associated with the pod, such as name & namespace   
          labels: 
            app : webapp          
              tier: frontend            
                environment: prod              
                  kubernetes.io/cluster-service: "true"    
                 type: LoadBalancer     
        spec:         # Specifications associated with pod such as how to run the container  
          containers:  # Containers are used by K8S for creating Pods   
            - name : webapp           # Name of a particular application/container in this deployment.     For example, 'webApp' is our app here     
              imagePullPolicy : IfNotPresent       # Pulls the container images from registry if not present already  
                image: my-image          # The Docker Image to use for creating Pods   
                  ports:         # Container Port Mappings    
                    - name: http        # Name of a particular port in this pod.  For example, 'http' is our app here     
                      containerPort :  80       # Expose the application on that specific PORT number inside Pods   
                        protocol : TCP         # Protocol to use for communication between Container and Host machine
